other_observers: Sue Riffe on 01/16/2006 Cole Wild, Bill Eads, Mary Magruder on 01/17/2006; Roger Sleeper is the original finder and identifier.  On 01/17/2006, this bird was seen also by Eric DeFonso and Sol Miller.
description: Very large gull with white head and body and tail; Faint brown streaks on crown and nape. Dark gray back and wings except for white scapular crescent, white tertail crescent, and black primaries with small white tips. The outer primary had a larger terminal white "mirror". Legs and feet pale pink (although perhaps brighter pink than most GBBG). Eye (iris) ochre, with red orbital ring. Bill yellow with subterminal dark smudge, with some red on lower mandible. Gonydeal angle pronounced, with a thicker gonys than expected for GBBG.Behaviors: Loafing on ice, swimming in water, bathing, stealing food from accompanying gulls, flying.Call: not heardPlumage: Sub adult probably 4th cycle
